questions.questionText: In Dynamics 365 Business Central, what must you configure before posting any financial transactions?
options: A. Warehouse locations.
B. The chart of accounts.
C. Manufacturing routings.
D. Service management contracts.
questions.explanation: The chart of accounts is the foundation of financial management and must be configured before any financial transactions can be posted, as all G/L entries reference accounts in the chart. Warehouse locations (A) are for inventory management and are not prerequisites for financial posting. Manufacturing routings (C) are for production processes. Service contracts (D) are for service management operations.

questions.topic: Configure Financials - Journals and Bank Accounts
questions.questionText: Which journal type in Business Central is used to post transactions directly to general ledger accounts, bank accounts, customer accounts, and vendor accounts?
options: A. Item journal.
B. General journal.
C. Job journal.
D. Resource journal.
questions.explanation: The general journal is used to post transactions directly to G/L accounts, bank accounts, customer, vendor, and fixed asset accounts. It is the most versatile journal type. Item journals (A) are specifically for inventory adjustments and item tracking. Job journals (C) track job-related entries for project management. Resource journals (D) track resource usage and time entries.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of dimensions in Business Central?
options: A. To define the physical size of products.
B. To add analytical tags to transactions for reporting and analysis.
C. To set up user security permissions.
D. To configure printing layouts for documents.
questions.explanation: Dimensions are analytical tags (such as department, project, or region) that can be attached to transactions to enable multi-dimensional reporting and analysis without creating separate G/L accounts. They do not relate to physical product size (A), which would be item attributes. User permissions (C) are set up through the security model. Print layouts (D) are configured through report layout management.

questions.questionText: What is the correct document flow for a standard sales process in Business Central?
options: A. Sales Invoice > Sales Order > Sales Quote.
B. Sales Quote > Sales Order > Sales Invoice.
C. Sales Credit Memo > Sales Invoice > Sales Order.
D. Sales Return Order > Sales Quote > Sales Invoice.
questions.explanation: The standard sales flow is: Sales Quote (optional proposal to customer) > Sales Order (confirmed customer order) > Sales Invoice (billing document). Each document can be converted to the next in the sequence. Reversing the order (A) goes against the natural business flow. Starting with credit memos (C) or returns (D) represents exception processes, not the standard sales cycle.

questions.questionText: When you post a purchase order in Business Central with the Receive and Invoice option, which ledger entries are created?
options: A. Only general ledger entries.
B. Item ledger entries, value entries, vendor ledger entries, and G/L entries.
C. Only vendor ledger entries.
D. Only item ledger entries.
questions.explanation: Posting a purchase order with Receive and Invoice creates item ledger entries (inventory receipt), value entries (cost valuation), vendor ledger entries (accounts payable), and corresponding G/L entries (financial posting). It is not limited to only G/L entries (A), only vendor entries (C), or only item entries (D) as the posting creates a complete set of interconnected ledger records.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: On an item card in Business Central, which field determines how the item's cost is calculated?
options: A. Base Unit of Measure.
B. Costing Method.
C. Item Category Code.
D. Shelf No.
questions.explanation: The Costing Method field (FIFO, LIFO, Average, Standard, Specific) determines how the cost of the item is calculated and applied to inventory transactions and value entries. Base Unit of Measure (A) defines the measurement unit for transactions. Item Category Code (C) is for item classification and filtering. Shelf No. (D) indicates the physical storage location.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: Which feature in Business Central allows you to track individual items using serial numbers or lot numbers?
options: A. Bin management.
B. Item tracking.
C. Stockkeeping units.
D. Item charges.
questions.explanation: Item tracking enables serial number and lot number tracking on items throughout the supply chain, from receipt to shipment. Bin management (A) is for organizing warehouse storage locations. Stockkeeping units (C) define location-specific item variants with different planning parameters. Item charges (D) allocate additional costs like freight to purchase or sales transactions.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: In Business Central, what is the minimum configuration required to enable basic warehouse functionality for a location?
options: A. Enable Directed Put-away and Pick.
B. Enable Require Receive and Require Shipment on the location card.
C. Set up bins and zones.
D. Configure warehouse employees only.
questions.explanation: Basic warehouse functionality requires enabling Require Receive and/or Require Shipment on the location card to create warehouse receipt and shipment documents. Directed Put-away and Pick (A) is the most advanced warehouse configuration level. Bins and zones (C) are optional for basic setup and required only for advanced warehousing. Warehouse employees (D) must be set up but alone do not enable warehouse document functionality.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: What does a production BOM (Bill of Materials) define in Business Central?
options: A. The routing operations for manufacturing an item.
B. The list of components and quantities needed to produce a finished item.
C. The vendors who supply raw materials.
D. The sales price of the finished product.
questions.explanation: A production BOM defines the components (materials) and their quantities required to produce a finished item, establishing the product structure hierarchy. Routing operations (A) define the manufacturing process steps and work centers. Vendor information (B) is on vendor cards and purchase records. Sales prices (D) are configured in price lists or the item card.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: What does a routing define in Business Central manufacturing?
options: A. The delivery route for shipping products.
B. The sequence of operations and work centers required to produce an item.
C. The approval workflow for purchase orders.
D. The network path for data synchronization.
questions.explanation: A routing defines the sequence of manufacturing operations, the work or machine centers where they are performed, and the time required for each step. It is not about delivery routes (A), which are shipping configurations. Approval workflows (C) are configured through the workflow engine. Network paths (D) are IT infrastructure, not manufacturing concepts.

questions.questionText: In Business Central service management, what does a service item represent?
options: A. A product available for sale.
B. A customer's item that requires servicing, repair, or maintenance.
C. A resource assigned to perform work.
D. A type of general ledger account.
questions.explanation: A service item represents a customer's item (equipment, machine, etc.) that requires servicing, repair, or maintenance tracking over its lifecycle. It differs from a sales item (A) which is a product in the catalog. A resource (C) is a person or machine that performs work. A G/L account (D) is a financial posting account.

questions.questionText: In Business Central, how are jobs structured for project management?
options: A. Jobs contain job tasks, which contain job planning lines.
B. Job planning lines contain jobs, which contain tasks.
C. Tasks are independent and not linked to jobs.
D. Jobs can only contain one task each.
questions.explanation: Jobs in Business Central have a hierarchical structure: Jobs contain Job Tasks, which contain Job Planning Lines (budget, billable, or both). This three-level hierarchy enables structured project tracking. Planning lines do not contain jobs (B) as the hierarchy flows downward. Tasks are always linked to a parent job (C). Jobs can contain multiple tasks (D) for complex projects.

questions.topic: Perform Operations - Process Payments and Journals
questions.questionText: What is the purpose of bank account reconciliation in Business Central?
options: A. To create new bank accounts.
B. To match bank statement entries with posted bank ledger entries and resolve differences.
C. To print bank checks.
D. To set up electronic payments.
questions.explanation: Bank reconciliation matches imported bank statement lines with posted bank ledger entries to ensure the bank balance in Business Central matches the actual bank balance, identifying and resolving discrepancies. It does not create bank accounts (A), which is done on the bank account card. Check printing (C) is a separate payments function. Electronic payment setup (D) is configured in payment methods.

questions.questionText: What do customer posting groups determine in Business Central?
options: A. The discount percentage for each customer.
B. The general ledger accounts to which customer transactions are posted.
C. The shipping method for deliveries.
D. The credit limit for customers.
questions.explanation: Customer posting groups map customer transactions to the appropriate G/L accounts (receivables account, payment discount accounts, etc.) ensuring proper financial posting. Discounts (A) are configured in price lists and discount setups. Shipping methods (C) are on the customer card or shipment agent setup. Credit limits (D) are a field on the customer card.

questions.questionText: Which two fields on the vendor card affect how purchase transactions are posted to the general ledger? (Choose two.)
options: A. Vendor Posting Group.
B. Gen. Bus. Posting Group.
C. Country/Region Code.
D. Phone No.
questions.explanation: The Vendor Posting Group (A) determines the payables G/L account. The Gen. Bus. Posting Group (B), combined with the Gen. Prod. Posting Group on items, determines the purchase and COGS accounts via the general posting setup matrix. Country/Region Code (C) affects address formatting and tax but not directly the G/L posting accounts. Phone No. (D) is contact information with no financial posting impact.

questions.questionText: Which depreciation method reduces the book value of a fixed asset by a constant percentage each year?
options: A. Straight-Line.
B. Declining-Balance.
C. Units of Production.
D. Manual.
questions.explanation: The Declining-Balance method applies a constant percentage to the remaining book value each year, resulting in higher depreciation in early years and lower in later years. Straight-Line (A) spreads cost evenly over the asset's useful life. Units of Production (C) bases depreciation on actual usage or output. Manual (D) requires manual journal entries for each depreciation period.

questions.topic: Perform Operations - Process Payments and Journals
questions.questionText: Which journal in Business Central is used to record the results of a physical inventory count?
options: A. General journal.
B. Physical inventory journal.
C. Revaluation journal.
D. Reclassification journal.
questions.explanation: The physical inventory journal is specifically designed to record physical count results and calculate adjustments between the counted quantity and the system quantity, posting positive or negative adjustments. General journals (A) are for G/L entries. Revaluation journals (C) adjust item costs/values. Reclassification journals (D) move items between locations, dimensions, or bins.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: What advanced warehouse feature requires the setup of zones and bins with bin types?
options: A. Inventory pick.
B. Warehouse shipment.
C. Directed put-away and pick.
D. Internal movement.
questions.explanation: Directed put-away and pick is the most advanced warehouse configuration in Business Central, requiring zones, bins with bin types, and bin rankings for automated storage and retrieval decisions. Inventory picks (A) are for basic warehouse operations without bin types. Warehouse shipments (B) require Require Shipment but not bin types. Internal movements (C) are simpler warehouse operations.

questions.questionText: Which two posting groups must be configured in the VAT posting setup to determine how VAT is calculated and posted? (Choose two.)
options: A. VAT Business Posting Group.
B. VAT Product Posting Group.
C. Inventory Posting Group.
D. Customer Price Group.
questions.explanation: The VAT posting setup uses the combination of VAT Business Posting Group (A) assigned to customers/vendors and VAT Product Posting Group (B) assigned to items/G/L accounts to determine the VAT calculation type, rate, and G/L accounts. Inventory posting groups (C) map inventory transactions to G/L accounts but are separate from VAT. Customer price groups (D) are for pricing, not tax configuration.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Pricing and Discounts
questions.questionText: In Business Central, where do you configure special prices for specific customers or customer groups?
options: A. On the item card's General FastTab.
B. In the Sales Price List or Price List Lines.
C. In the General Posting Setup.
D. On the location card.
questions.explanation: Sales prices for specific customers or customer groups are configured in Price Lists and Price List Lines using the new pricing experience. This allows different prices based on customer, quantity, date range, and currency. The item card (A) shows the default unit price but not customer-specific pricing. General Posting Setup (C) is for G/L account mapping. Location cards (D) are for warehouse configuration.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of the requisition worksheet in Business Central?
options: A. To track employee time sheets.
B. To plan and generate purchase orders based on demand and reorder policies.
C. To reconcile bank statements.
D. To manage fixed asset depreciation.
questions.explanation: The requisition worksheet helps plan procurement by calculating supply requirements based on demand forecasts, sales orders, and reorder policies, then generating purchase orders automatically. It is not for time sheets (A), which are in job management. Bank reconciliation (C) has its own dedicated process. Asset depreciation (D) is managed through the fixed assets module.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: What are item charges used for in Business Central?
options: A. To charge customers a penalty fee.
B. To allocate additional costs (like freight or insurance) to purchase or sales transactions.
C. To apply finance charges on overdue invoices.
D. To calculate employee commissions.
questions.explanation: Item charges allow you to allocate additional costs such as freight, insurance, and handling to the cost of purchased or sold items, adjusting the item's inventory value accurately. They are not penalty fees (A), which would be finance charges. Finance charges (C) on overdue invoices are a separate accounts receivable function. Commission calculations (D) are handled through separate commission setups.

questions.questionText: How does Business Central handle transactions in foreign currencies?
options: A. Foreign currency transactions are not supported.
B. Transactions are recorded in the foreign currency and converted to the local currency using exchange rates.
C. All transactions must be manually converted before entry.
D. Only the Euro is supported as a foreign currency.
questions.explanation: Business Central supports multi-currency transactions by recording amounts in the foreign currency and converting to the local currency (LCY) using the exchange rates defined in the currency exchange rate table. Foreign currencies are fully supported (A). Conversion is automatic (C), not manual. Multiple currencies are supported (D), not just the Euro.

questions.questionText: Which production order status allows you to make changes to the order before releasing it to the shop floor?
options: A. Released.
B. Finished.
C. Planned or Firm Planned.
D. Simulated.
questions.explanation: Planned and Firm Planned production orders allow modifications to components, routing, and quantities before being released to the shop floor. Released orders (A) have been sent to production and are more restrictive. Finished orders (B) are completed and closed. Simulated orders (D) are for what-if scenarios and do not post any entries or participate in planning.

questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of a service order in Business Central?
options: A. To order items from a vendor.
B. To document and manage service requests, including repair and maintenance work.
C. To transfer inventory between locations.
D. To process employee expense claims.
questions.explanation: A service order documents and manages customer service requests including repairs, maintenance, spare parts, and labor tracking. Purchase orders (A) are for vendor ordering. Transfer orders (C) move inventory between locations. Expense claims (D) are not processed through service orders.

questions.questionText: In Business Central, what type of job planning line should you create to track both the budget and the amount to invoice the customer?
options: A. Budget line type.
B. Billable line type.
C. Both Budget and Billable line type.
D. None; budgets and billing are tracked separately.
questions.explanation: The 'Both Budget and Billable' line type tracks both the budgeted cost and the amount that can be invoiced to the customer on a single planning line, enabling comparison of planned vs. actual. Budget-only (A) tracks costs without billing capability. Billable-only (B) tracks invoicing without budget comparison. Both functions can be combined on one line (D is incorrect).

questions.questionText: What must be set up to require manager approval before a purchase order can be posted in Business Central?
options: A. A number series.
B. An approval workflow.
C. A posting group.
D. A dimension value.
questions.explanation: An approval workflow defines the rules for requiring approval before documents like purchase orders can be posted, including the approval chain and conditions. Number series (A) generate document numbers automatically. Posting groups (C) map transactions to G/L accounts. Dimension values (D) are analytical tags for reporting.

questions.questionText: What is a blanket sales order used for in Business Central?
options: A. A one-time sale to a new customer.
B. A framework agreement for recurring deliveries over time, from which individual sales orders are created.
C. An internal transfer of goods.
D. A request for quotation sent to vendors.
questions.explanation: A blanket sales order is a framework agreement with a customer for recurring deliveries over a defined period, from which individual sales orders are created as needed. It is not for one-time sales (A), which use standard sales orders. Internal transfers (C) use transfer orders. Vendor quotations (D) are purchase documents.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: When should you create stockkeeping units (SKUs) for an item in Business Central?
options: A. When the item is sold to multiple customers.
B. When the item needs different replenishment or planning parameters at different locations.
C. When the item has serial number tracking.
D. When the item is a service type.
questions.explanation: Stockkeeping units (SKUs) allow you to define location-specific replenishment methods, vendors, lead times, and planning parameters for the same item at different locations or with different variants. Multiple customers (A) does not require SKUs. Serial tracking (C) is configured through item tracking codes. Service items (D) do not require SKUs.

questions.questionText: In a warehouse with Require Receive enabled, what document is used to receive items from a purchase order?
options: A. Inventory put-away.
B. Warehouse receipt.
C. Transfer order.
D. Assembly order.
questions.explanation: When Require Receive is enabled on the location card, items from purchase orders are received using a warehouse receipt document, which provides an intermediate receiving step. Inventory put-aways (A) are used in basic warehouse setups without Require Receive. Transfer orders (C) move items between locations. Assembly orders (D) combine components into finished items.

questions.questionText: What feature in Business Central enables automatic posting of transactions between related companies?
options: A. Consolidation.
B. Intercompany postings.
C. Currency exchange.
D. Cost accounting.
questions.explanation: Intercompany postings allow related companies in Business Central to exchange transactions (journals, sales/purchase documents) automatically, eliminating manual re-entry and ensuring both sides stay in balance. Consolidation (A) combines financial statements from multiple companies for reporting. Currency exchange (C) handles multi-currency conversion. Cost accounting (D) distributes costs internally within a company.

questions.questionText: Which two documents can you use to process a sales return in Business Central? (Choose two.)
options: A. Sales credit memo.
B. Sales return order.
C. Purchase credit memo.
D. Service credit memo.
questions.explanation: A sales credit memo (A) processes simpler customer returns with immediate financial reversal. A sales return order (B) provides more control with separate receive and invoice steps for complex returns. Purchase credit memos (C) are for vendor returns, not customer returns. Service credit memos (D) are for service transactions specifically.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of deferral templates in Business Central?
options: A. To defer posting of journal entries to a future date.
B. To spread revenue or expense recognition across multiple accounting periods.
C. To postpone payment to vendors.
D. To delay inventory counting.
questions.explanation: Deferral templates allow you to automatically spread revenue or expense recognition across multiple accounting periods according to a defined schedule, supporting accrual accounting principles. They do not defer journal posting dates (A). Vendor payment postponement (C) is managed through payment terms. Inventory counting delays (D) are operational decisions, not financial deferrals.

questions.questionText: How can you create a single purchase invoice for multiple posted purchase receipts from the same vendor?
options: A. By using the Combine Receipts function.
B. By manually copying each receipt.
C. This is not possible in Business Central.
D. By deleting the receipts and creating a new order.
questions.explanation: The Combine Receipts function creates a single purchase invoice from multiple posted receipts for the same vendor, streamlining the invoicing process when a vendor sends one invoice for multiple deliveries. Manual copying (B) is inefficient and error-prone. This functionality is fully available in Business Central (C is incorrect). Deleting receipts (D) is destructive and not the correct approach.

questions.topic: Configure Sales and Purchasing - Inventory Setup
questions.questionText: Which entity in Business Central manufacturing represents the physical location where production operations are performed?
options: A. Production BOM.
B. Work center.
C. Item journal.
D. Vendor card.
questions.explanation: A work center represents a physical production location (a group of machines or workers) where manufacturing operations are performed, with defined capacity and costs. Production BOMs (A) list components needed. Item journals (C) record inventory transactions. Vendor cards (D) store supplier information.

questions.questionText: What does a service contract in Business Central provide?
options: A. A template for sales quotes.
B. An agreement defining service levels, response times, and periodic invoicing for service items.
C. A vendor purchase agreement.
D. An employment contract for service technicians.
questions.explanation: A service contract defines the terms of service including service levels, response times, and periodic invoicing schedules for specific service items belonging to a customer. It is not a sales quote template (A). Purchase agreements (C) are vendor-related blanket orders. Employment contracts (D) are HR documents outside Business Central's service management.

questions.questionText: What does the General Posting Setup matrix define in Business Central?
options: A. The tax rates for different regions.
B. The G/L accounts used when posting transactions based on the combination of Gen. Bus. and Gen. Prod. Posting Groups.
C. The approval hierarchy for documents.
D. The number series for all documents.
questions.explanation: The General Posting Setup defines which G/L accounts are used when posting transactions, based on the combination of General Business Posting Group and General Product Posting Group. This matrix drives the financial posting logic. Tax rates (A) are in VAT posting setup. Approval hierarchies (C) are in workflow setup. Number series (D) are configured separately in the number series setup.

questions.questionText: What is the Assemble-to-Order feature in Business Central used for?
options: A. Assembling items in advance for stock.
B. Assembling items on demand linked directly to a sales order line.
C. Disassembling returned items.
D. Ordering assembly services from a vendor.
questions.explanation: Assemble-to-Order links an assembly order directly to a sales order line, assembling the item specifically for that customer order when the sales order is posted. Assemble-to-Stock (A) is for building inventory in advance. Disassembly (C) is not a standard assembly feature. Vendor assembly ordering (D) is a subcontracting scenario, not assemble-to-order.

questions.questionText: What does the Work in Process (WIP) calculation for jobs accomplish in Business Central?
options: A. It calculates employee payroll.
B. It recognizes revenue and costs for ongoing jobs at the end of a financial period.
C. It generates purchase orders for job materials.
D. It schedules job tasks on a calendar.
questions.explanation: The WIP calculation for jobs recognizes revenue and costs for ongoing projects according to the selected WIP method (cost value, sales value, percentage of completion, etc.) at the end of an accounting period for accurate financial reporting. It does not calculate payroll (A). It does not generate purchase orders (C). It does not schedule tasks (D).

questions.questionText: Which two financial reports are standard in Business Central? (Choose two.)
options: A. Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position).
B. Income Statement (Profit and Loss).
C. Social media engagement report.
D. Website traffic analytics report.
questions.explanation: The Balance Sheet (A) and Income Statement (B) are standard financial reports in Business Central generated from G/L entries using financial reports (formerly account schedules). They are required for statutory reporting. Social media engagement (C) and website traffic (D) are marketing analytics not part of ERP financial reporting.

questions.topic: Set up Business Central - Create and configure a company
questions.questionText: When creating a new company in Business Central, which method provides a guided step-by-step process for initial configuration?
options: A. Manually creating all setup tables.
B. Using Assisted Setup wizards.
C. Importing a SQL database backup.
D. Copying the production database.
questions.explanation: Assisted Setup wizards guide users step-by-step through initial company configuration including chart of accounts, payment terms, posting groups, and other essential settings. Manual setup (A) is possible but time-consuming and error-prone. SQL database import (C) is not a supported company creation method in SaaS. Database copying (D) creates duplicates rather than clean new companies.

questions.topic: Set up Business Central - Create and configure a company
questions.questionText: What is the purpose of a configuration package in Business Central?
options: A. To install new extensions.
B. To export and import setup data and master data between companies or environments for rapid deployment.
C. To create user accounts.
D. To print financial reports.
questions.explanation: Configuration packages allow you to export setup data, master data, and configuration from one company and import it into another, enabling rapid deployment and consistent setup across companies. Extension installation (A) is done through the Extension Management page. User accounts (C) are created in the admin center. Report printing (D) is a separate function.

questions.questionText: In Business Central, what is the purpose of permission sets?
options: A. To define price lists for customers.
B. To define which database objects (tables, pages, reports) a user or group can access and what operations they can perform.
C. To configure posting groups.
D. To set up number series.
questions.explanation: Permission sets define granular access rights to database objects including tables, pages, reports, and codeunits, specifying which CRUD (create, read, update, delete) operations users can perform. Price lists (A) are sales configuration. Posting groups (C) are financial configuration. Number series (D) are document numbering configuration.

questions.questionText: Which two methods can you use to assign permissions to users in Business Central? (Choose two.)
options: A. Assign permission sets directly to individual users.
B. Assign permission sets through security groups.
C. Send an email requesting access.
D. Post a request in the general journal.
questions.explanation: Permissions can be assigned directly to individual users (A) for specific needs, or through security groups (B) for efficient management of users with similar roles. Both methods are supported in Business Central. Email requests (C) are not a system mechanism for permissions. General journals (D) are for financial posting, not security management.

questions.topic: Set up Business Central - Core functionality
questions.questionText: What is the purpose of job queues in Business Central?
options: A. To manage customer service queues.
B. To schedule and run background tasks and reports at specified times or intervals.
C. To organize warehouse picking orders.
D. To prioritize sales orders.
questions.explanation: Job queues allow you to schedule background processing of reports, codeunits, and other tasks at specified times or recurring intervals without user intervention. Customer service queues (A) are in CRM applications. Warehouse picking (C) is managed through warehouse documents. Sales order priority (D) is handled through order processing rules.

questions.topic: Set up Business Central - Core functionality
questions.questionText: Which feature in Business Central allows users to set up automated email sending for documents like posted sales invoices?
options: A. General journal templates.
B. Email accounts and document sending profiles.
C. Dimension values.
D. Posting groups.
questions.explanation: Email accounts define the SMTP/Exchange connection, and document sending profiles configure how documents are sent to customers (email, print, or electronic document). General journal templates (A) define journal structures. Dimension values (C) are analytical tags. Posting groups (D) map to G/L accounts.

questions.questionText: In Business Central, what is the difference between global dimensions and shortcut dimensions?
options: A. There is no difference.
B. Global dimensions can be used as filters on ledger entries and in analysis views, while shortcut dimensions are additional dimensions displayed as columns on journal lines.
C. Global dimensions are for manufacturing only.
D. Shortcut dimensions replace global dimensions.
questions.explanation: Global dimensions (two maximum) are stored directly on ledger entries and can be used as filters in reports and analysis views for fast performance. Shortcut dimensions (up to eight total including globals) appear as columns on journal and document lines for easy entry. They are distinct concepts (A). Global dimensions apply to all areas (C). Shortcut dimensions complement, not replace, global dimensions (D).

questions.questionText: What happens when you set up a blocking combination of dimension values in Business Central?
options: A. Users can still post transactions with that combination but receive a warning.
B. The system prevents posting any transaction that uses the blocked combination of dimension values.
C. The dimension values are deleted.
D. The blocked combination is only enforced in reports.
questions.explanation: Blocking dimension combinations prevents users from posting transactions that use specific invalid combinations, enforcing data integrity at the posting level. It does not just warn (A) - it blocks the posting entirely. Dimension values are not deleted (C). The blocking is enforced during posting (D), not just in reports.

questions.questionText: When setting up a vendor account in Business Central, which configuration determines the payment timing for vendor invoices?
options: A. Gen. Bus. Posting Group.
B. Payment Terms Code.
C. Vendor Posting Group.
D. Currency Code.
questions.explanation: The Payment Terms Code defines the payment due date calculation, discount date, and discount percentage for vendor invoices, determining when payments are due. Gen. Bus. Posting Group (A) affects G/L account mapping. Vendor Posting Group (C) determines the payables G/L account. Currency Code (D) specifies the transaction currency.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of the payment registration page in Business Central?
options: A. To create new customer accounts.
B. To quickly register customer payments against outstanding invoices from a single page.
C. To set up bank accounts.
D. To configure payment methods.
questions.explanation: The payment registration page provides a simplified interface for registering customer payments against outstanding invoices, making it easy to match payments to specific invoices. Creating customer accounts (A) is done on the customer card. Bank account setup (C) is on the bank account card. Payment method configuration (D) is in the payment methods setup.

questions.questionText: What is the difference between personalizing and customizing pages in Business Central?
options: A. They are the same thing.
B. Personalizing changes the page for the individual user only; customizing (by an admin) changes the page for a profile/role affecting all users with that profile.
C. Personalizing changes pages for all users; customizing is for individuals.
D. Neither can change page layouts.
questions.explanation: Personalizing allows individual users to adjust their own page layout (adding/removing fields, rearranging). Customizing is done by administrators and changes the page for an entire user profile/role, affecting all users assigned to that profile. They are distinct concepts (A). The scope is not reversed (C). Both can change page layouts (D).

questions.questionText: What does the 'Inspect pages and data' feature allow users to do in Business Central?
options: A. Delete records from tables.
B. View the underlying table name, field names, and current values of data displayed on a page.
C. Edit source code.
D. Create new extensions.
questions.explanation: The Inspect pages and data feature (Ctrl+Alt+F1) shows the underlying table name, field names, field numbers, and values for the data displayed on the current page, useful for troubleshooting and configuration. It does not allow deleting records (A), editing code (C), or creating extensions (D).

questions.questionText: When processing a sales order, how can you check if sufficient inventory is available to fulfill the order?
options: A. Only by checking the warehouse manually.
B. Using the Item Availability feature which shows current stock, expected receipts, and allocations.
C. By posting the order and checking for errors.
D. Item availability cannot be checked before shipping.
questions.explanation: The Item Availability feature shows current inventory, scheduled receipts (from purchase orders and production), gross requirements (from sales orders and production), and projected available balance. Manual warehouse checks (A) are unnecessary with system availability. Posting to check errors (C) is a destructive approach. Availability checking is a core feature (D) available before posting.

questions.questionText: How do you correct a posted sales invoice that contains an error in Business Central?
options: A. Delete the posted invoice.
B. Use the Correct or Cancel function on the posted sales invoice, which creates a corrective credit memo.
C. Edit the posted invoice directly.
D. Ignore the error.
questions.explanation: Business Central provides Correct and Cancel functions on posted sales invoices. Correct creates a credit memo and a new corrected invoice. Cancel creates a credit memo only. Posted invoices cannot be deleted (A) or edited directly (C) as they are legal documents. Ignoring errors (D) leads to incorrect financial records.

questions.questionText: What is the over-receipt feature used for in Business Central purchasing?
options: A. To reject deliveries that exceed the ordered quantity.
B. To allow receiving more items than ordered within a configured tolerance percentage.
C. To return excess items to the vendor.
D. To cancel purchase orders.
questions.explanation: The over-receipt feature allows users to receive quantities exceeding the ordered amount within a predefined tolerance percentage, accommodating situations where vendors ship slightly more than ordered. It is not a rejection mechanism (A). Returns (C) are processed separately through purchase return orders or credit memos. Order cancellation (D) is a different process.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of prepayment invoices in Business Central?
options: A. To record final payments.
B. To invoice and collect a deposit or advance payment from a customer before the full order is shipped and invoiced.
C. To process vendor refunds.
D. To close fiscal periods.
questions.explanation: Prepayment invoices allow businesses to invoice customers for a deposit or advance payment (a percentage or fixed amount) before the goods are shipped or the full invoice is created. Final payments (A) are processed through regular invoices. Vendor refunds (C) use purchase credit memos. Fiscal period closing (D) is an accounting process, not a document type.

questions.topic: Perform Operations - Process Payments and Journals
questions.questionText: What is the purpose of recurring journals in Business Central?
options: A. To post one-time transactions.
B. To automate the posting of transactions that repeat at regular intervals, such as monthly rent or depreciation.
C. To reconcile bank statements.
D. To create new customers.
questions.explanation: Recurring journals allow you to set up journal lines that are posted automatically at regular intervals (daily, weekly, monthly, etc.) for repetitive transactions like rent, subscriptions, or accruals. One-time transactions (A) use standard journals. Bank reconciliation (C) has its own process. Customer creation (D) is master data management.

questions.topic: Perform Operations - Process Payments and Journals
questions.questionText: Which two actions can you perform using the Edit in Excel feature in Business Central? (Choose two.)
options: A. View and filter Business Central data in Excel.
B. Edit data in Excel and publish changes back to Business Central.
C. Create new extensions.
D. Modify page layouts.
questions.explanation: Edit in Excel allows users to open Business Central data in Excel for viewing, filtering, and analysis (A), and also to modify data and publish changes back to Business Central (B) using the Excel Add-in. Extension creation (C) is a developer task done in Visual Studio Code. Page layout modification (D) is done through personalization or customization.

questions.questionText: Which Microsoft product integration allows Business Central users to share and collaborate on documents stored alongside their Business Central records?
options: A. Microsoft Power BI.
B. Microsoft OneDrive for Business.
C. Microsoft Dynamics 365 Sales.
D. Microsoft Power Automate.
questions.explanation: OneDrive for Business integration allows users to open, share, and manage files directly from Business Central records, including drag-and-drop file management. Power BI (A) provides analytics and dashboards. Dynamics 365 Sales (C) provides CRM integration. Power Automate (D) provides workflow automation.

questions.topic: Perform Operations - Process Payments and Journals
questions.questionText: What is the purpose of the Adjust Exchange Rates batch job in Business Central?
options: A. To change the local currency of the company.
B. To adjust the LCY value of foreign currency balances on customer, vendor, and bank accounts when exchange rates change.
C. To delete currency records.
D. To block foreign currency transactions.
questions.explanation: The Adjust Exchange Rates batch job recalculates the local currency (LCY) equivalent of outstanding foreign currency balances (on customer, vendor, bank, and G/L accounts) based on current exchange rates, posting unrealized gains or losses. It does not change the local currency (A). It does not delete currencies (C) or block transactions (D).

questions.questionText: Which two components must be configured when setting up fixed assets in Business Central? (Choose two.)
options: A. Depreciation books to define depreciation rules and posting behavior.
B. Fixed asset posting groups to map transactions to G/L accounts.
C. Sales price lists for fixed assets.
D. Warehouse bins for fixed asset storage.
questions.explanation: Depreciation books (A) define the depreciation method, useful life, and whether to post to G/L. Fixed asset posting groups (B) specify which G/L accounts receive acquisition, depreciation, and disposal entries. Sales price lists (C) are for inventory items, not fixed assets. Warehouse bins (D) are for inventory management, not fixed asset tracking.

questions.topic: Perform Operations - Process Fixed Asset Transactions
questions.questionText: What are the three main transaction types in the fixed asset lifecycle in Business Central?
options: A. Sales, returns, and credits.
B. Acquisition, depreciation, and disposal.
C. Receipt, put-away, and pick.
D. Quote, order, and invoice.
questions.explanation: The fixed asset lifecycle consists of acquisition (purchasing/recording the asset), depreciation (allocating the cost over useful life), and disposal (selling or retiring the asset). Sales, returns, and credits (A) are sales document types. Receipt, put-away, and pick (C) are warehouse operations. Quote, order, and invoice (D) are the sales document flow.

questions.questionText: What is the Dimension Correction Tool used for in Business Central?
options: A. To create new dimensions.
B. To update or correct dimension values on already posted ledger entries.
C. To delete all dimensions.
D. To export dimensions to Excel.
questions.explanation: The Dimension Correction Tool allows administrators to correct dimension values on posted ledger entries without reversing and reposting transactions, fixing analytical tagging errors retroactively. Creating dimensions (A) is done in the Dimensions setup page. Deleting all dimensions (C) is not the tool's purpose. Excel export (D) is done through the Edit in Excel feature.

questions.questionText: What does the data analysis mode in Business Central allow users to do?
options: A. Write custom AL code.
B. Analyze and explore data directly within list pages using pivot-like functionality without leaving Business Central.
C. Create new database tables.
D. Configure posting groups.
questions.explanation: Data analysis mode lets users analyze data directly on list pages with pivot-like capabilities including grouping, filtering, and aggregating data without exporting to Excel or using separate BI tools. Writing AL code (A) requires Visual Studio Code. Creating tables (C) is a developer task. Posting groups (D) are configured in the financial setup.

questions.questionText: What are security filters used for in Business Central?
options: A. To filter spam emails.
B. To restrict a user's access to specific records within a table based on field values, such as limiting access to a specific company department's data.
C. To configure firewall rules.
D. To filter report output.
questions.explanation: Security filters restrict which records within a table a user can access based on field values, enabling row-level security. For example, limiting a user to only see transactions for their department. Email filtering (A) is not a Business Central security feature. Firewall rules (C) are network infrastructure. Report filtering (D) is done through report request pages, not security filters.
